title: T3 Refactor worktree.lua to tab-local :tcd primary + <leader>uw workspace-create
description: Refactor lua/lib/worktree.lua from global :cd single-worktree model to tab-local :tcd so worktree tabs coexist as parallel workspaces. Change switch_to_worktree (cd@451 -> tab-targeting + tcd), stop tearing down buffers/LSP on switch (close_worktree_buffers@298/reset_plugin_state@386/restart_lsp@334), add M.create_workspace using 'wt switch --create <branch> --yes --format=json' (via _worktrunk_json@25) -> new tab -> tcd -> sidekick show({name=claude}). Add <leader>uw keymap. Behavior change to daily-driver switcher; interactive verify required. Part of epic dotfiles_nvim-tabs-sidebar-7yp.
acceptance_criteria: switch_to_worktree uses tab-local :tcd not global :cd; teardown trio not invoked on tab-local switch; M.create_workspace creates worktree+tab+tcd+launches sidekick Claude; <leader>uw mapped; headless require('lib.worktree') and require('lib.tablist') exit 0; existing public signatures preserved.
notes: T3 + T3.1 CODE COMPLETE, statically verified (orchestrator read-back of all regions). Project-picker flow: list_projects@449 (zoxide -> .git roots only), _worktrunk_json@28 (opts.cwd via vim.system, backward compat), create_workspace@536 (project-pick -> branch -> wt cwd=project -> open_in_tab -> sidekick). All earlier tab-local fixes confirmed. REMAINING: user live functional verify (headless can't exercise wt/UI; MCP bridge disconnected). Minor follow-ups for T6: delete_worktree@860 guard uses raw == not norm_path; create_for_ticket/switch_to_pr still run wt in global cwd (acceptable - always called in-repo).

Part of epic dotfiles_nvim-tabs-sidebar-7yp.
acceptance_criteria: All 8 questions answered with file:line or 'not found'; output sufficient to render T2-T4 prompts without further discovery.
notes:
T1 COMPLETE. Findings:
Q1: Tabs-sidebar ALREADY landed on main in commit 42b3a5c (tablist.lua new, worktree.lua modified); main nvim tree clean. Worktree ~/.dotfiles.nvim-tabs-sidebar (branch nvim-tabs-sidebar @149897a) is BEHIND main and its uncommitted edits REVERT the sidebar (snacks.lua deletes gh_pr block, options.lua -52 lines, which-key lazy restructure, keymaps.lua identical). DO NOT merge/apply the worktree. Workstream A = effectively done.
Q2: tabs source lua/lib/tablist.lua (M.source@66, open@115, toggle@125, refresh@135); registered snacks.lua:53 'tabs = require(lib.tablist).source'.
Q3: lua/lib/worktree.lua EXISTS, large worktrunk API (switch_to_worktree@409, create_worktree_from_remote@678, create_for_ticket@1041, switch_to_pr@861, _worktrunk_json@25).
Q4: sidekick require('sidekick.cli').show({name='claude',focus=true}) does NOT take cwd/env at call time; cwd=getcwd(0) at session creation (session/init.lua:100), env from tool config (terminal.lua:282). => :tcd the tab THEN show() launches Claude in that cwd. No low-level plumbing needed.
Q5: existing wt cmds: 'wt list --format=json'; 'wt switch pr:N --format=json'; 'wt switch --create <branch> --yes --format=json' (worktree.lua:1079, via _worktrunk_json@25-31 systemlist, synchronous). Worktree path template: {{repo_path}}/../{{repo}}.{{branch|sanitize}} default; HFS project override {{repo_path}}/{{branch|sanitize}}. post-start hook: 'wt step copy-ignored'.
Q6: settings.json (NOT a symlink; tracked at claude/.claude/settings.json) hooks.SessionStart has 2 entries (claude-capture async 5s; bd prime), matcher ''. Hook output mechanism (stdout vs additionalContext) UNVERIFIED.
Q7: <leader>u taken: <leader>ur (keymaps.lua:107), <leader>ut (keymaps.lua:110). <leader>uw free.
Q8: nvim 0.12 per stored memory (>=0.10 ok); not re-run this session.
ARCHITECTURE CONFLICT: existing worktree.lua uses GLOBAL :cd (line 451) + buffer-close/LSP-restart = one-worktree-at-a-time. User's tab-workspace vision needs per-tab :tcd. Decision pending.

title: Research: sidekick.nvim per-tab env injection and per-workspace cwd (tcd)
description: For the same 'Neovim-aware Claude Code session' design, we need sidekick.nvim to (1) inject env vars into the spawned Claude Code CLI job (so a tabpage/workspace identifier + opt-in flag reach the SessionStart hook), and (2) ideally establish a per-workspace cwd (tab-local :tcd) so each Claude session's cwd is its worktree. Inspect sidekick.nvim source (installed under the vim.pack packpath) to determine both capabilities and the exact config/injection point.
acceptance_criteria: Determined whether sidekick exposes per-tool/per-session env injection (with config key + file:line), and whether/how it sets the spawned session cwd (tab-local tcd vs global). Source cited file:line. Concrete injection point identified for passing CLAUDE_NVIM_* vars.
notes:
sidekick.nvim v2.3.0 (CHANGELOG.md:3). Findings from search-agent source inspection (file:line cited; not independently re-read by orchestrator).

ENV INJECTION: YES, per-tool. Config field sidekick.cli.Config.env at lua/sidekick/cli/init.lua:17 (table<string,string|false>). Merged at lua/sidekick/cli/terminal.lua:282 via vim.tbl_extend('force',{},os_environ(),tool.config.env,tool.env,{NVIM,TERM,...}) -> sidekick itself sets NVIM last. Passed to jobstart at terminal.lua:296. env[key]=false unsets (terminal.lua:291-295).

CWD: session cwd = opts.cwd or vim.fn.getcwd(0) at lua/sidekick/cli/session/init.lua:100. getcwd(0) honors window/tab-local :tcd, falling back to global. Passed to jobstart cwd at terminal.lua:297. Session identity = tool+cwd hash (session/init.lua:104-108) => distinct cwd yields distinct session.

IMPLICATION: inject CLAUDE_NVIM_* via tool config.env; rely on getcwd(0)+:tcd per tab so each session cwd = its worktree. Both requirements satisfied.
close_reason: Research complete; env-injection + cwd behavior determined and recorded in notes.

title: Research: does the neovim MCP server support tabpage/window/buffer-scoped queries?
description: For the 'Neovim-aware Claude Code session' design (one nvim instance, tabs as workspaces, one Claude session per tab), the linchpin is whether mcp__neovim__* tools can be scoped to a specific tabpage/window/buffer or whether they are global/active-buffer-only. If active-only, background sessions will read whatever tab the USER is focused on (cross-tab cross-talk), and scoping must fall back to cwd filtering. Identify the server source/package, inspect each tool's schema, and determine scoping capability.
acceptance_criteria: For each mcp__neovim__ tool, classified as (a) global, (b) active-window/buffer, or (c) accepts an explicit tabpage/window/buffer scope param. Source cited file:line. Clear yes/no conclusion: can a query be pinned to a specific tabpage.
notes:
VERIFIED from live MCP tool schemas loaded into this session (authoritative for the connected server version). The scoping primitive is the BUFFER (number or file path); NO tool exposes a tabpage or window parameter.

Per-tool classification:
- nvim_list_buffers: GLOBAL (no params) -> returns ALL buffers across ALL tabs. Entries include file name/path => filterable by cwd prefix.
- nvim_get_cursor_position: ACTIVE-only (no params), 'what are you looking at?' -> follows the user's focused window. UNRELIABLE for a background session in a shared instance. Avoid.
- nvim_get_diagnostics: optional buffer (number|path) + severity; omit = all buffers.
- nvim_get_document_symbols / nvim_lsp_hover / nvim_lsp_definition / nvim_get_lsp_status: optional buffer (number|path); default to CURRENT (active) buffer when omitted.
- nvim_get_buffer_content: REQUIRES buffer (number|path).

CONCLUSION: Cannot pin a query to a tabpage/window. CAN pin to a buffer by file path. => Workspace scoping must use CWD/FILE-PATH filtering: always pass an explicit buffer under the session cwd, filter nvim_list_buffers by cwd prefix, never rely on nvim_get_cursor_position or omitted-buffer defaults (they follow user focus = wrong tab).
close_reason: Research complete; scoping capability determined and recorded in notes.

title: lua_ls: typed signatures from lazydev-injected plugin libraries not resolving
description:
Hovering/go-to-definition on plugin functions accessed via require() (e.g. cli.toggle where local cli = require('sidekick.cli')) produces no typed signature in the nvim config, even though lazydev correctly injects the plugin's lua dir into lua_ls workspace.library. Goal: get typed signatures (sidekick.cli.toggle(opts?: sidekick.cli.Show) + the fun(name:string) overload) to resolve for lazydev-injected plugin requires.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open ~/.config/nvim/lua/kickstart/plugins/sidekick.lua (which has 'local cli = require("sidekick.cli")').
2. Put cursor on cli.toggle and run :lua vim.lsp.buf.definition() -> 'No locations found'.
3. Hover (K) on cli.toggle -> no typed signature.
Contrast: opening ~/.local/share/nvim/site/pack/core/opt/sidekick.nvim/lua/sidekick/cli/init.lua and hovering M.toggle (~line 100) DOES show the typed signature. So lua_ls reads the annotations but does not resolve the require() into the library.
design:
CONFIRMED working (do not re-investigate):
- nvim uses native vim.pack (NOT lazy.nvim). sidekick.nvim at ~/.local/share/nvim/site/pack/core/opt/sidekick.nvim.
- lazydev attaches ONLY to clients named EXACTLY 'lua_ls' or 'emmylua_ls' (hardcoded, not configurable) -- lazydev/lsp.lua:6, buf.lua:31-50. Client was 'luals' (from lsp/luals.lua filename) so lazydev never attached. FIXED: renamed lsp/luals.lua -> lsp/lua_ls.lua and init.lua:132 'luals' -> 'lua_ls'.
- lazydev now WORKS: ':LazyDev' shows sidekick.nvim/lua and snacks.nvim/lua injected into Lua.workspace.library.
- Added lazydev library entry { path='sidekick.nvim', mods={'sidekick'} } in kickstart/plugins/lazydev.lua (belt-and-suspenders).
- sidekick.cli.Show defined at cli/init.lua:25-29 (fields name?, focus?, filter?, all?); toggle annotated at :98-100.

REMAINING SYMPTOM: lua_ls has the library CONFIGURED but has not SCANNED it into its require map. Direct-open works (parsed on open); cross-module require resolution fails.
acceptance_criteria: In a normal nvim config file, go-to-definition on cli.toggle jumps to sidekick/cli/init.lua and hover shows 'sidekick.cli.toggle(opts?: sidekick.cli.Show)' plus the fun(name:string) overload. Typed signatures resolve generally for lazydev-injected plugin requires.
notes:
NEXT DIAGNOSTIC STEPS (unfinished):
1. Confirm lua_ls finished async library preload before testing (':lua =vim.lsp.status()'). Library list is large. Retry go-to-def once idle.
2. Cross-check require('snacks') go-to-def (snacks.nvim/lua also injected). snacks works but sidekick fails => sidekick-specific. Both fail => general runtime-injected-library scan problem.
3. Restart lua_ls (no :LspRestart in native LSP): :lua vim.lsp.stop_client(vim.lsp.get_clients({name='lua_ls'}), true) then :edit. Let it fully load, then test.
4. Suspect lua_ls 3.15.0 does not re-scan library dirs added mid-session via lazydev didChangeConfiguration poke; clean client start or lua_ls cache clear may be needed.
Highest-ranked hypothesis: async preload timing / runtime-added library not scanned.
